examples.title: Pseudocode: Thresholding
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Thresholding
INPUT: image, threshold
OUTPUT: binary_image

BEGIN
 FOR EACH pixel IN image
 IF pixel > threshold THEN
 binary_image[pixel] = 255
 ELSE
 binary_image[pixel] = 0
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode applies thresholding to an image by iterating over each pixel and setting its value to 255 if it's above the threshold, and 0 otherwise.

examples.title: Python: Thresholding
examples.description: Applies thresholding to an image using Python
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image

# Load the image
img = Image.open('image.jpg')

# Convert the image to grayscale
gray_img = img.convert('L')

# Apply thresholding
threshold = 127
binary_img = np.where(gray_img > threshold, 255, 0)

# Print the segmented image
print(binary_img)
examples.explanation: This Python code loads an image, converts it to grayscale, applies thresholding using the 127 threshold value, and prints the resulting binary image.

examples.title: Python: Edge Detection
examples.description: Applies edge detection to an image using Python
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
import cv2

# Load the image
img = Image.open('image.jpg')

# Convert the image to grayscale
gray_img = img.convert('L')

# Apply edge detection
edges = cv2.Canny(np.array(gray_img), 50, 150)

# Print the edges
print(edges)
examples.explanation: This Python code loads an image, converts it to grayscale, applies edge detection using the Canny algorithm, and prints the resulting edges.
